rat.headline: Friction and Distraction
rat.lead: Rat faces the steepest year-pillar headwind in the 60-year cycle. Fire melts the Water-Rat's resources; the Horse year amplifies that destruction. Most Rats will feel real friction, and a meaningful share will face hard choices around money and relationships. The few Rats who navigate this well are the ones who slow down, prepare, and refuse to take risks they don't need to take.
rat.career: Career headwinds. Most Rats should not pursue major changes this year — keep your job, defer big moves to 2027. Channel your strategic mind into focused, narrow execution rather than scattered ambition.
rat.money: Possibly the worst Horse-year sign for money. Bill specifically calls out water Rats: stay defensive, avoid speculation, and protect what you have. The few Rats who do well are the ones treating 2026 as a year to fortify, not expand.
rat.love: Friction and distraction. Your partner's needs start feeling like obligations rather than desires. Slow down and tend the relationship — emotional distance compounds quietly until it can't be ignored.
rat.advice: Slow down. The Rats who navigate this year well do less, not more — and the less they do, the better they do it.

ox.headline: Stubbornness Meets Speed
ox.lead: Ox in the Fire Horse year is structurally one of the toughest fits. The Ox's deliberate pace clashes with the Horse's speed; the Fire element drains the Ox's Water nature. Most Oxen will feel pulled in directions they don't want to go. The Oxen who do well this year adapt their pace consciously — they don't fight the Horse, they ride it just enough.
ox.career: Don't initiate change — sustain what's working. Most Oxen who push for new opportunities will overextend. The Oxen who hold position and execute their existing role at high quality come out best.
ox.money: Same headwind as the Rat — Water-Ox energy clashes with double fire. Avoid major financial commitments. Keep cash defensively. This is not the year to bet on yourself in a big way.
ox.love: Your tendency to dig in reads as coldness in a year that demands warmth. If you don't adapt to the Horse's speed, resentment builds on both sides. Singles: your deliberate approach is a liability — promising connections fizzle while you're still deciding.
ox.advice: Adapt your pace just enough to ride the year, not fight it. Stubbornness is the trap.

tiger.headline: Passion Without an Anchor
tiger.lead: Tiger is one of the four signs structurally aligned with the Fire Horse year. Bold, prepared Tigers will see major openings in career and relationships. The risk: amplified intensity becomes burnout if not channeled. Most Tigers will end the year ahead. The few who stumble are the ones who confused momentum with judgment.
tiger.career: Volunteer for visible projects. Step up to lead. Bold and prepared Tigers can become the example for their organization. The trap is overextension — pick fewer, bigger bets rather than chasing every opening.
tiger.money: Tigers who can take risks (with preparation) will do well. Bold Tigers in entrepreneurial roles or sales-driven careers are favored. Watch for burnout — the year makes you feel like your energy is unlimited; it isn't.
tiger.love: Passion year. Tigers in good relationships will see major moves — proposals, marriages, babies. Singles will have plenty of attraction, but discipline matters for follow-through.
tiger.advice: Channel your intensity, don't detonate it. Pick fewer, bigger bets.

rabbit.headline: Hidden Clash, Handle With Care
rabbit.lead: Rabbit's deliberate, careful nature is at odds with the Fire Horse's speed. The pace itself is the obstacle. Rabbits who slow down and trust their instincts will be fine; Rabbits who try to keep up will exhaust themselves. The good months are real — Snake month and Sheep month are particularly favorable.
rabbit.career: Don't try to keep up with faster signs. Let your careful judgment be the asset — slow, deliberate, accurate work outperforms hustle for the Rabbit this year. Specialize, don't generalize.
rabbit.money: Rabbit has a lucky money star this year that softens the headwind. Conservative investments outperform speculation. Avoid impulse purchases; the year's tempo will push you toward decisions you'll regret.
rabbit.love: Trust slowly, verify quietly. Connections may start fast and fizzle just as fast. Your natural caution is your greatest asset here — don't apologize for it.
rabbit.advice: Slow is your speed. Don't apologize for it.

dragon.headline: Ego Is the Enemy
dragon.lead: Dragon does well structurally in the Fire Horse year, particularly in career and visible work. The watch-out is the ego: the year's energy feeds the Dragon's natural confidence, and that confidence overflows into relationships in unhelpful ways. Most Dragons will have a strong career year; the personal-life results are more variable.
dragon.career: Strong career year. Visible opportunities, leadership roles, ambitious projects. The Fire Horse rewards confidence — lead. The watch-out: don't bulldoze coworkers; the same intensity that wins career battles loses team trust.
dragon.money: Good for ambitious financial moves and visible projects. Dragons in leadership roles will see direct gains. Avoid arrogant overreach — most Dragon failures this year will come from believing your own hype.
dragon.love: Ego is the enemy. The year strokes your confidence and your confidence costs you in relationships. You may pour yourself into work and become emotionally unavailable before you notice. A slow leak you won't acknowledge until the damage is done.
dragon.advice: Keep score on relationships, not just on results.

snake.headline: Depleted and Reactive
snake.lead: Snake is coming off its own year (2025) at full power, and the Fire Horse hits while the Snake is depleted. The double-fire intensifies arguments that Snakes would normally sidestep. Most Snakes will feel reactive and short-fused; the strongest Snakes use the year for inward consolidation rather than outward conquest.
snake.career: Don't initiate big career changes — sustain. Your strategic patience is more valuable than aggressive moves this year. Stay deep in your existing expertise rather than pivoting.
snake.money: Mixed. Long-game investments are fine; speculative moves are not. Conserve energy and capital — your reserves matter this year.
snake.love: Pause before you speak. The double fire amplifies arguments you'd normally sidestep. Singles: your intuition is unreliable when depleted — don't commit to patterns just because they look familiar.

horse.headline: It's Your Year. Don't Waste It.
horse.lead: Horse in its own year is structurally amplified — your nature is everywhere, and that's the problem. The temptation to mistake momentum for direction is the central risk. Most Horses will end the year having gained energy and lost focus. The Horses who do well treat 2026 as a year for one or two big bets, not a hundred small ones.
horse.career: High visibility, lots of energy, lots of distractions. Pick one or two big bets, not a hundred small ones. The Horses who scatter end the year tired with nothing finished.
horse.money: Strong if you stay focused. Horses with one clear venture will do well; Horses dabbling in five things will end the year tired and broke.
horse.love: Don't waste this year. Long-running grievances will get worse if you ride past them. Hold up a mirror — what's the honest state of your relationship? Singles: prime conditions, but the year makes commitment feel like a cage. Be careful — there may be a hidden gem worth keeping.
horse.advice: Concentration is your superpower this year. Spread thin and you lose.

sheep.headline: Sensitivity Meets Instability
sheep.lead: Sheep is the Horse's soulmate sign — one of the four signs structurally aligned with the Fire Horse year. Romance, wealth, and recognition all show up. The risk for Sheep is sensitivity without grounding: in a polarized year, emotional swings become destabilizing. Most Sheep will have one of the best years of the decade.
sheep.career: Strong career year. Soulmate-of-Horse status means alignment with the year's energy. Lean into creative and design-oriented work — fire favors flair. Visible work pays off.
sheep.money: Predicted to be one of the best financial years of all signs. Most Sheep will see real gains. Watch out: those who stumble could fall hard — don't get cocky, especially around speculative bets.
sheep.love: Genuinely good year for romance. Major moves likely — proposals, marriages, babies. Watch out for being drawn to emotional intensity that's unstable rather than alive.
sheep.advice: Distinguish 'alive' from 'unstable.' The line is thinner this year.

monkey.headline: Isolation and Grief
monkey.lead: Monkey faces real pressure in the Fire Horse year. The Monkey's strategic, calculating nature doesn't align with the Horse's speed and directness. There is also an unlucky star factor that compounds the year-pillar headwind. Most Monkeys will struggle; the Monkeys who do well stay focused on a single domain (often technology) and resist scattering.
monkey.career: Real career pressure. Monkeys who can maintain laser focus on a single domain — often technology — can still do well. Monkeys who scatter or play strategic games will struggle.
monkey.money: Difficult year. Avoid speculation. Monkey's clever financial maneuvering doesn't work in a year that rewards directness. Steady salaried income outperforms creative bets.
monkey.love: Drop the games. The Horse year doesn't have time for strategy in relationships. If you're single, look in the mirror first — pursuing romance from loneliness produces bad choices this year.
monkey.advice: Direct beats clever in 2026. Pick one focus and execute.

rooster.headline: Control Versus Connection
rooster.lead: Rooster is a relatively favorable year-pillar fit, with one condition: Roosters who try to control their partners will lose them. The year favors Roosters who direct their precision inward (work, craft) rather than outward (relationships). Most Roosters will see good career outcomes; relationships are more conditional.
rooster.career: Decent career year. Roosters' natural precision and structure work well in chaos. Direct your control inward — toward work, craft, expertise — rather than toward managing other people.
rooster.money: Steady. Disciplined Roosters do fine; perfectionist Roosters who delay decisions miss opportunities. The year rewards execution over polish.
rooster.love: Control yourself, not your partner. Loyalty is your asset — let it show. Singles: loosen your filters slightly. Some of what you're rejecting is real compatibility.
rooster.advice: Precision in work, generosity in relationships.

dog.headline: Loyalty Under Pressure
dog.lead: Dog is the strongest year-pillar fit of all twelve signs. Calculated, strategic, loyal Dogs do exceptionally well in the Fire Horse year across every life domain. The watch-out: martyrdom. Don't absorb others' chaos while leaving your own needs unspoken. Most Dogs will end the year materially and personally ahead.
dog.career: Best career fit of all signs. Dogs are calculated, strategic, and trustworthy — exactly what the Fire Horse year rewards. Major opportunities. Watch for taking on more than you can deliver well.
dog.money: Strong year. Dogs in stable roles will see solid gains. Bold-but-calculated Dogs in entrepreneurial roles will do exceptionally well. Trust your judgment.
dog.love: Best relationship year for Dogs. Reliability becomes magnetic in a chaotic year. Watch for martyrdom — don't absorb your partner's chaos while leaving your own needs unspoken. Singles: the right connection comes to you. Don't force it.
dog.advice: Loyalty is your superpower. Use it without losing yourself.

pig.headline: Rocky Phases and Real Tension
pig.lead: Pig is in a rocky-but-workable year. Water-element nature meets double-fire, which is a real mismatch — but luck is technically returning to the Pig after a difficult Snake year. Most Pigs will see real ups and real downs. The Pigs who do well lean into their natural sensuality and warmth rather than trying to operate at the year's aggressive pace.
pig.career: Mixed. Don't initiate major career changes — Pigs tend to go with the flow rather than prepare, and unprepared moves fail in a Fire Horse year. Steady contribution outperforms ambitious pivots.
pig.money: Coming off a difficult Snake year. Luck is technically returning but slowly. Conservative is the right register — small consistent gains, not big swings.
pig.love: Rocky phases and real tension. Get yourself on solid footing first. Lean into your natural sensuality — it's your asset, but only when you're grounded. Relationships pursued from a shaky foundation won't stick.
pig.advice: Prepare more than you think you need to. Then move.
